mnemonics: Half Adders are HALF-way to a full adder (they can't handle carry-in from previous bits).
keyPoints: A half adder adds two single binary digits and produces a sum and a carry output.
It has no input to account for a carry from a less significant bit addition.
The circuit is built using an XOR gate for the sum and an AND gate for the carry.
commonMistakes: Attempting to use a half adder for multi-bit addition, which requires the ability to accept a carry-in.
Confusing the half adder's truth table with that of a full adder, which has three inputs.
